pattern: The reflected value of each number is just a function you can compute once per element, so the whole 'sort by reflection, break ties by original value' rule reduces to an ordinary sort with a two-part key.
hints: The binary reflection of a number does not depend on any other number in the array, so you can compute it once per element ahead of the sort.
Reversing a binary string and reading it back as a number is a direct, mechanical operation -- build the reversed digit string and parse it back in base two.
Sort using a composite key: the reflection value first, and the original value second to break ties, exactly as described.

edge_inputs.description: statement example 1, a tie broken by original value
statement example 2, two different numbers share a reflection
a power of two reflects down to one
already-palindromic binary values sort by their own value since reflections equal themselves
the maximum allowed value alongside the minimum
many repeats of the same value

stress.description: random arrays at the maximum allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
